ai_foundations.answer: Artificial intelligence is software that can perform tasks that usually need human-like thinking, such as understanding language, recognizing images, making predictions, or helping write code. Modern AI is powered by data, algorithms, and models trained to find patterns.
Generative AI creates new content such as text, code, images, audio, video, or designs. It learns patterns from training data and uses those patterns to generate useful outputs from prompts.

A large language model, or LLM, is an AI model trained on huge amounts of text and code. It predicts the next useful tokens in a response, which lets it answer questions, write, summarize, translate, and help with programming.
Prompt engineering means writing clear instructions for an AI model. A strong prompt usually includes the goal, context, constraints, output format, and examples.

Machine learning is a way to build software that learns patterns from data instead of being programmed with every rule by hand. It is used for recommendations, predictions, vision, speech, fraud detection, and many AI systems.
A neural network is a machine learning model inspired by connected neurons. It uses layers of mathematical units to learn patterns from data, such as language, images, sound, or sensor readings.
Computer vision is AI that understands images or video. It can classify images, detect objects, read text, follow movement, inspect products, and help robots understand the physical world.
Robotics combines hardware, software, sensors, control systems, and AI so machines can move and act in the real world. AI helps robots perceive, plan, and make decisions.

models_companies.answer: OpenAI builds AI models and products such as ChatGPT, GPT models, Codex, and Sora. GPT models are used for reasoning, writing, coding, analysis, tool use, and multimodal AI workflows.
Anthropic builds Claude, a family of AI assistants known for writing, analysis, coding help, long-context tasks, and safety-focused design.
Google DeepMind builds advanced AI systems including Gemini, AlphaFold, and multimodal models. Gemini can work across text, code, images, audio, and video depending on the product and model.
xAI builds Grok, an AI model family designed for conversational AI and product integrations. Like other frontier systems, it should be used with verification for important facts.
Hugging Face is a major platform for open-source AI models, datasets, demos, and machine learning tools. Developers use it to discover models, host Spaces, and build AI applications.
Llama is Meta's open model family. Developers use Llama for local AI apps, private deployments, fine-tuning, research, and open-source experiments.
Mistral AI builds efficient open and commercial AI models. It is popular with developers who care about strong performance, lower latency, and flexible deployment.
DeepSeek models are known for coding, reasoning, and cost-conscious AI experimentation. For production, teams should review licensing, security, hosting, and compliance needs.

programming.answer: Python is a beginner-friendly programming language used for AI, web apps, automation, data science, scripting, and backend services. Its simple syntax and huge library ecosystem make it a strong first language.
JavaScript powers interactive websites and many full-stack applications. It runs in browsers and also on servers through Node.js.
Flask is a lightweight Python web framework. It is good for building APIs, dashboards, prototypes, and beginner-friendly web apps because routing and templates are easy to understand.
React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces with components. It is commonly used for dashboards, SaaS apps, AI tools, and mobile-like web experiences.
An API is a contract that lets software systems communicate. A web API usually exposes endpoints that send and receive data, often as JSON.
Software engineering is the discipline of building reliable software with good architecture, readable code, tests, version control, documentation, deployment, and maintenance.

infrastructure.answer: Linux is an operating system widely used for servers, development, cloud infrastructure, cybersecurity, and DevOps. Learning the terminal, files, permissions, processes, and networking is very useful.
Docker packages an application and its dependencies into containers. Containers help apps run consistently across laptops, servers, and cloud platforms.
Kubernetes manages containers across clusters of machines. It handles deployment, scaling, service discovery, health checks, and rolling updates for production systems.
Cloud computing lets teams rent servers, databases, storage, networking, and AI services instead of owning physical data centers. AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud are major cloud platforms.
DevOps combines development and operations practices so teams can ship software faster and more reliably. CI/CD pipelines automatically test, build, and deploy code.
Cybersecurity protects systems, networks, data, and users from attacks. Safe learning focuses on defensive skills, secure coding, permissions, encryption, authentication, and legal practice labs.

productivity_startups.answer: A startup is a company built to find a repeatable, scalable business model. Great startups usually focus on a painful problem, a clear user, fast learning, and strong execution.
Productivity tools help people work faster and stay organized. AI productivity tools can summarize, draft, search, schedule, automate workflows, and help teams make decisions.
Open source projects share their source code publicly so people can use, inspect, improve, and contribute. It is a powerful way to learn real software engineering.
Data science uses statistics, programming, visualization, and machine learning to find insights in data and support better decisions.
